briefdescription.para: Channel number for Last Aux channel.

sectiondef.header: Indexes for reading the Calibration Coefficient Data.
Indexes for reading the Minimum/Maximum Measurement Data.
Averaging to be done for the channels.

briefdescription.para: Power Down ADC A and ADC B.
This macro checks if the XADC device is in Event Sampling mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device is in Event Sampling Mode.
FALSE if the device is in Continuous Sampling Mode.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the XADC device is in External Mux mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device is in External Mux Mode.
FALSE if the device is NOT in External Mux Mode.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts XADC Raw Data to Temperature(centigrades).

parameterdescription.para: is the Raw ADC Data from XADC.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts XADC/ADC Raw Data to Voltage(volts).

parameterdescription.para: is the XADC/ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Temperature in centigrades to XADC/ADC Raw Data.

parameterdescription.para: is the Temperature in centigrades to be converted to XADC/ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Voltage in Volts to XADC/ADC Raw Data.

parameterdescription.para: is the Voltage in volts to be converted to XADC/ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro is used for writing to the XADC Registers using the command FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the value to be written to XADC register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XAdcPs_WriteFifo(XAdcPs *InstancePtr, u32 Data);

briefdescription.para: This macro is used for reading from the XADC Registers using the data FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID.
The table XAdcPs_ConfigTable contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the ID of the device for which the device configuration pointer is to be returned.

listitem.para: A pointer to the configuration found.
NULL if the specified device ID was not found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XAdcPs device/instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the XADC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
points to the XAdcPs device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XAdcPs_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XAdcPs_CfgInitialize() API.

briefdescription.para: The functions sets the contents of the Config Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The functions reads the contents of the Config Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Config Register. Use the XADCPS_SR_*_MASK constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The functions reads the contents of the Miscellaneous Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Miscellaneous Status Register. Use the XADCPS_MSTS_*_MASK constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: The functions sets the contents of the Miscellaneous Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the 32 bit data to be written to the Register.

briefdescription.para: The functions reads the contents of the Miscellaneous control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Config Register. Use the XADCPS_SR_*_MASK constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the XADC Hard Macro in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Xxadc instance.

briefdescription.para: Get the ADC converted data for the specified channel.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the channel number. Use the XADCPS_CH_* defined in the file xadcps.h. The valid channels are
0 to 6
13 to 31

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the ADC converted data for the specified channel. The XADC Monitor/ADC device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of the 16 data read from the device.
The channels 7,8,9 are used for calibration of the device and hence there is no associated data with this channel.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the calibration coefficient data for the specified parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
specifies the calibration coefficient to be read. Use XADCPS_CALIB_* constants defined in xadcps.h to specify the calibration coefficient to be read.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the calibration coefficient. The XADC device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of the 16 data read from the device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Minimum/Maximum measurement for one of the specified parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Use XADCPS_MAX_* and XADCPS_MIN_* constants defined in xadcps.h to specify the parameters (Temperature, VccInt, VccAux, VBram, VccPInt, VccPAux and VccPDro).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
specifies the parameter for which the Minimum/Maximum measurement has to be read. Use XADCPS_MAX_* and XADCPS_MIN_* constants defined in xadcps.h to specify the data to be read.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the maximum/minimum measurement for specified parameter. The XADC device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of the 16 data read from the device.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of samples of averaging that is to be done for all the channels in both the single channel mode and sequence mode of operations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the number of samples of averaging programmed to the Configuration Register 0. Use the XADCPS_AVG_* definitions defined in xadcps.h file :
XADCPS_AVG_0_SAMPLES for no averaging
XADCPS_AVG_16_SAMPLES for 16 samples of averaging
XADCPS_AVG_64_SAMPLES for 64 samples of averaging
XADCPS_AVG_256_SAMPLES for 256 samples of averaging

briefdescription.para: This function returns the number of samples of averaging configured for all the channels in the Configuration Register 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para:
The averaging read from the Configuration Register 0 is returned. Use the XADCPS_AVG_* bit definitions defined in xadcps.h file to interpret the returned value :
XADCPS_AVG_0_SAMPLES means no averaging
XADCPS_AVG_16_SAMPLES means 16 samples of averaging
XADCPS_AVG_64_SAMPLES means 64 samples of averaging
XADCPS_AVG_256_SAMPLES means 256 samples of averaging

memberdef.argsstring: (XAdcPs *InstancePtr, u8 Channel, int IncreaseAcqCycles, int IsEventMode, int IsDifferentialMode)
memberdef.name: XAdcPs_SetSingleChParams

briefdescription.para: The function sets the given parameters in the Configuration Register 0 in the single channel mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the channel number for the single channel mode. The valid channels are 0 to 6, 8, and 13 to 31. If the external Mux is used then this specifies the channel oonnected to the external Mux. Please read the Device Spec to know which channels are valid.

is a boolean parameter which specifies whether the Acquisition time for the external channels has to be increased to 10 ADCCLK cycles (specify TRUE) or remain at the default 4 ADCCLK cycles (specify FALSE). This parameter is only valid for the external channels.
specifies whether the operation of the ADC is Event driven or Continuous mode.
is a boolean parameter which specifies unipolar(specify FALSE) or differential mode (specify TRUE) for the analog inputs. The input mode is only valid for the external channels.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the Configuration Register 0.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled or the input parameters are not valid for the selected channel.
The number of samples for the averaging for all the channels is set by using the function XAdcPs_SetAvg.

The calibration of the device is done by doing a ADC conversion on the calibration channel(channel 8). The input parameters IncreaseAcqCycles, IsDifferentialMode and IsEventMode are not valid for this channel

briefdescription.para: This function enables the alarm outputs for the specified alarms in the Configuration Register 1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit-mask of the alarm outputs to be enabled in the Configuration Register 1. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will be disabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing XADCPS_CFR1_ALM_*_MASK and XADCPS_CFR1_OT_MASK masks defined in xadcps_hw.h.

simplesect.para: The implementation of the alarm enables in the Configuration register 1 is such that the alarms for bit positions of 1 will be disabled and alarms for bit positions of 0 will be enabled. The alarm outputs specified by the AlmEnableMask are negated before writing to the Configuration Register 1.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the status of the alarm output enables in the Configuration Register 1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: This is the bit-mask of the enabled alarm outputs in the Configuration Register 1. Use the masks XADCPS_CFR1_ALM*_* and XADCPS_CFR1_OT_MASK defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value. Bit positions of 1 indicate that the alarm output is enabled. Bit positions of 0 indicate that the alarm output is disabled.

The implementation of the alarm enables in the Configuration register 1 is such that alarms for the bit positions of 1 will be disabled and alarms for bit positions of 0 will be enabled. The enabled alarm outputs returned by this function is the negated value of the the data read from the Configuration Register 1.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified calibration in the Configuration Register 1 :

listitem.para: XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_ADC_OFFSET_MASK : Calibration 0 -ADC offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_ADC_GAIN_OFFSET_MASK : Calibration 1 -ADC gain and offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_PS_OFFSET_MASK : Calibration 2 -Power Supply sensor offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_PS_GAIN_OFFSET_MASK : Calibration 3 -Power Supply sensor gain and offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_DISABLE_MASK : No Calibration

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the Calibration to be applied. Use XADCPS_CFR1_CAL*_* bits defined in xadcps_hw.h. Multiple calibrations can be enabled at a time by oring the XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_ADC_* and XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_PS_* bits. Calibration can be disabled by specifying XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_DISABLE_MASK;

briefdescription.para: This function reads the value of the calibration enables from the Configuration Register 1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para:
The value of the calibration enables in the Configuration Register 1 :
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_ADC_OFFSET_MASK : ADC offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_ADC_GAIN_OFFSET_MASK : ADC gain and offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_PS_OFFSET_MASK : Power Supply sensor offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_PS_GAIN_OFFSET_MASK : Power Supply sensor gain and offset correction
XADCPS_CFR1_CAL_DISABLE_MASK : No Calibration

briefdescription.para: This function sets the specified Channel Sequencer Mode in the Configuration Register 1 :

para.verbatim: - Default safe mode (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SAFE) - One pass through sequence (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_ONEPASS) - Continuous channel sequencing (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_CONTINPASS) - Single Channel/Sequencer off (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SINGCHAN) - Simultaneous sampling mode (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SIMUL_SAMPLING) - Independent mode (XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_INDEPENDENT)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the sequencer mode to be set. Use XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_* bits defined in xadcps.h.

simplesect.para: Only one of the modes can be enabled at a time. Please read the Spec of the XADC for further information about the sequencer modes.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the channel sequencer mode from the Configuration Register 1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para:
The channel sequencer mode :
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SAFE : Default safe mode
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_ONEPASS : One pass through sequence
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_CONTINPASS : Continuous channel sequencing
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SINGCHAN : Single channel/Sequencer off
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_SIMUL_SAMPLING : Simultaneous sampling mode
XADCPS_SEQ_MODE_INDEPENDENT : Independent mode

briefdescription.para: The function sets the frequency of the ADCCLK by configuring the DCLK to ADCCLK ratio in the Configuration Register #2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is clock divisor used to derive ADCCLK from DCLK. Valid values of the divisor are
0 to 255. Values 0, 1, 2 are all mapped to 2. Refer to the device specification for more details

simplesect.para:
- The ADCCLK is an internal clock used by the ADC and is synchronized to the DCLK clock. The ADCCLK is equal to DCLK divided by the user selection in the Configuration Register 2.
There is no Assert on the minimum value of the Divisor.

briefdescription.para: The function gets the ADCCLK divisor from the Configuration Register 2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: The divisor read from the Configuration Register 2.
The ADCCLK is an internal clock used by the ADC and is synchronized to the DCLK clock. The ADCCLK is equal to DCLK divided by the user selection in the Configuration Register 2.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified channels in the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be disabled before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels to be enabled. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 will be enabled and bit mask of 0 will be disabled. The ChEnableMask is a 32 bit mask that is written to the two 16 bit ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the channel enable bits status from the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: Gets the channel enable bits. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels that are enabled and bit mask of 0 are the channels that are disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the averaging for the specified channels in the ADC Channel Averaging Enable Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be disabled before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which averaging is to be enabled. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Averaging will be enabled for bit masks of 1 and disabled for bit mask of 0. The AvgEnableChMask is a 32 bit mask that is written to the two 16 bit ADC Channel Averaging Enable Sequencer Registers.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Averaging Enables Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the channels for which the averaging has been enabled in the ADC Channel Averaging Enables Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: The status of averaging (enabled/disabled) for all the channels. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which averaging is enabled and bit mask of 0 are the channels for averaging is disabled

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Analog input mode for the specified channels in the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be disabled before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which the input mode is differential mode. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to specify the channel numbers. Differential input mode will be set for bit masks of 1 and unipolar input mode for bit masks of 0. The InputModeChMask is a 32 bit mask that is written to the two 16 bit ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Analog input mode for all the channels from the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: The input mode for all the channels. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH_* defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which input mode is differential and bit mask of 0 are the channels for which input mode is unipolar.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of Acquisition cycles in the ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be disabled before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which the number of acquisition cycles is to be extended. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Acquisition cycles will be extended to 10 ADCCLK cycles for bit masks of 1 and will be the default 4 ADCCLK cycles for bit masks of 0. The AcqCyclesChMask is a 32 bit mask that is written to the two 16 bit ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the Channel Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the status of acquisition from the ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: The acquisition time for all the channels. Use XADCPS_SEQ_CH__* defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which acquisition cycles are extended and bit mask of 0 are the channels for which acquisition cycles are not extended.

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the contents of the given Alarm Threshold Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the index of an Alarm Threshold Register to be set. Use XADCPS_ATR_* constants defined in xadcps.h to specify the index.
is the 16-bit threshold value to write into the register.

simplesect.para: Use XAdcPs_SetOverTemp() to set the Over Temperature upper threshold value.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the specified Alarm Threshold Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is the index of an Alarm Threshold Register to be read. Use XADCPS_ATR_* constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to specify the index.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the contents of the selected Alarm Threshold Register.

briefdescription.para: This function enables programming of the powerdown temperature for the OverTemp signal in the OT Powerdown register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables programming of the powerdown temperature for the OverTemp signal in the OT Powerdown register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: The function enables the Event mode or Continuous mode in the sequencer mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.
is a boolean parameter that specifies continuous sampling (specify FALSE) or event driven sampling mode (specify TRUE) for the given channel.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the sampling mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para:
The sampling mode
0 specifies continuous sampling
1 specifies event driven sampling mode

briefdescription.para: This function sets the External Mux mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

specifies whether External Mux is used
FALSE specifies NO external MUX
TRUE specifies External Mux is used
specifies the channel to be used for the external Mux. Please read the Device Spec for which channels are valid for which mode.

simplesect.para: There is no Assert in this function for checking the channel number if the external Mux is used. The user should provide a valid channel number.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Power Down mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

specifies the Power Down Mode
XADCPS_PD_MODE_NONE specifies NO Power Down (Both ADC A and ADC B are enabled)
XADCPS_PD_MODE_ADCB specifies the Power Down of ADC B
XADCPS_PD_MODE_XADC specifies the Power Down of both ADC A and ADC B.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Power Down mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para:
Mode specifies the Power Down Mode
XADCPS_PD_MODE_NONE specifies NO Power Down (Both ADC A and ADC B are enabled)
XADCPS_PD_MODE_ADCB specifies the Power Down of ADC B
XADCPS_PD_MODE_XADC specifies the Power Down of both ADC A and ADC B.

detaileddescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

The test
Resets the device,
Writes a value into the Alarm Threshold register and reads it back for comparison.
Resets the device again.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the value read from the Alarm Threshold register is the same as the value written.
XST_FAILURE Otherwise

simplesect.para: This is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer to the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

detaileddescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XADCPS_INTX_* bits defined in xadcps_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XADCPS_INTX_* bits defined in xadcps_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the enabled interrupts read from the Interrupt Mask Register (IPIER).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XADCPS_IPIXR_* constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the I.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register(IPISR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XADCPS_IPIXR_* constants defined in xadcps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the IPISR.
The device must be configured at hardware build time to include interrupt component for this function to work.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register (IPISR).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAdcPs instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XADCPS_IPIXR_* bits which are defined in xadcps_hw.h.
